machinery_genes.quote: The fourteen predicted TA modules and four orphan antitoxins as well as the loci encoding the Vbh T4SS (VbhB2-11), Dtr functions (TraA-D), and T4CP (TraG) were highlighted in detail.
machinery_genes.note: Vbh = VirB-homologous T4SS on plasmid pVbh of B. schoenbuchensis R1. The apparatus is named collectively as 'VbhB2-11' (VirB2-VirB11 homologs); VbhB2 = VirB2-family major pilin. Family role assigned from the T4SS apparatus brief VirB/D4 vocabulary. Paper names the cluster as a range, not gene-by-gene; per-gene functional quotes are not given.

machinery_genes.role: type IV coupling protein (T4CP; substrate-selecting receptor ATPase)
machinery_genes.quote: the loci encoding the Vbh T4SS (VbhB2-11), Dtr functions (TraA-D), and T4CP (TraG) were highlighted in detail
machinery_genes.note: TraG is explicitly named as the T4CP of the pVbh conjugation system. The T4CP is the VirD4-family coupling protein of the VirB/D4 archetype: it links the Dtr machinery to the translocation channel and selects substrates. Counted as channel-associated apparatus (coupling protein), distinct from the Dtr relaxase TraA which is the secreted substrate.

notes: System: the Vbh (VirB-homologous) T4SS, a conjugative type-IVA (VirB/D4 archetype) machinery encoded on plasmid pVbh of Bartonella schoenbuchensis R1 (genbank/RefSeq region NZ_CP019789.1). Harms et al. 2017 (PLoS Genet) characterised pVbh, showed it is a functional conjugative plasmid, and showed VbhT (a FicT-family FIC+BID toxin) is the interbacterial effector secreted via this Vbh T4SS. The sys_instance_label 'VbhB-VbhT' = the VbhB-named apparatus plus its VbhT effector. APPARATUS: the paper names the translocation machinery collectively as 'Vbh T4SS (VbhB2-11)' = VirB2-VirB11 homologs, plus the T4CP 'TraG'. I expanded VbhB2-11 into the individual VirB2-11-homolog genes (all sharing the same verbatim range quote); the paper does NOT give per-gene functional quotes, so each VbhB role is assigned from the T4SS apparatus brief VirB/D4 vocabulary - this is why the status is PARTIAL rather than COMPLETE (collective naming, not gene-by-gene characterisation). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED: (1) VbhB1 / VirB1 (lytic transglycosylase) - the named cluster is VbhB2-11, so no VbhB1 is asserted; (2) the Dtr / relaxasome genes TraA-D - TraA is the conjugative relaxase, which the paper shows is the SECRETED SUBSTRATE of the Vbh T4SS ('the conjugative relaxase TraA of pVbh ... was found to be translocated into recipient cells'), so per the curation rules it is substrate, not channel apparatus; TraB-D are DNA-processing (Dtr) functions, not the translocation channel; (3) VbhT and its antitoxin VbhA (the FicTA toxin-antitoxin module) - VbhT is the effector (instance context), VbhA is an antitoxin/chaperone, both non-machinery. LOW-CONFIDENCE: per-gene VbhB role assignments rest on the brief's VirB/D4 family map, not on individual quotes; the only verbatim apparatus naming is the 'VbhB2-11 / TraG' range. Engel 2011 included as a review establishing the VirB/Vbh T4SS family context in Bartonella. No automated detector, Pfam/HMM scan, or RefSeq mining was used; apparatus identity rests solely on Harms 2017.
